# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std/http/server.ts";

addEventListener("fetch", (event) => {
  const url = new URL(event.request.url);
  const modulePath = Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATH") || url.searchParams.get("modulePath");

  if (modulePath) {
    event.respondWith(handleRequest(modulePath));
  } else {
    event.respondWith(new Response("Module path not found.", { status: 404 }));
  }
});

async function handleRequest(modulePath: string): Promise<Response> {
  try {
    const module = await import(modulePath);
  
    if (typeof module.default !== "function") {
      return new Response("Module does not export a default function.", { status: 400 });
    }
  
    const result = module.default();
  
    return new Response(JSON.stringify(result), { headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" } });
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response(`Error importing module: ${error}`, { status: 500 });
  }
}
